purpose: Additional candidate pool for later HEI canonical batches while terminal access is unavailable. These candidates are not canonical records yet and require final duplicate checks and public-quality staging packages before append.

status_legend.A: Likely HEI-addable after one more source/duplicate pass
status_legend.B: Potential event/update candidate, usually active entity or service-level incident
status_legend.C: Hold / weak / likely non-HEI unless better evidence appears

candidates.headline: Bithumb accidentally credited users with about 620,000 BTC / USD 40-44B in a February 2026 promotional payout error, restricted affected trading and withdrawals within about 35 minutes, and recovered 99.7% of the erroneous internal ledger balances.
candidates.hei_relevance: Strong active-side CEX operational incident candidate. It is not a hack and not a shutdown, but it caused exchange-side price dislocation, account/trading/withdrawal restrictions, and regulatory scrutiny. Should be modeled as active with operational_error / trading_halted / withdrawal_suspended-style events using HEI's closest enums.

duplicate_check.result: no existing repository hit via connector search

candidates.next_action: Stage as active CEX with events: operational error / erroneous crediting, trading_halted or withdrawal_suspended for affected accounts, regulatory_action or other for FSC inspection. Do not mark hack or dead.

candidates.headline: BlockFills paused client deposits and withdrawals in February 2026 amid market stress while clients could reportedly still open and close spot and derivatives positions.
candidates.hei_relevance: Negative and trading-adjacent, but likely outside HEI v0 scope because BlockFills is more crypto lender/liquidity provider/institutional platform than public CEX/DEX. Keep as hold unless HEI explicitly adds institutional liquidity venues.

duplicate_check.result: no existing repository hit via connector search

candidates.next_action: Hold. Do not stage for HEI canonical unless the project scope expands beyond CEX/DEX/hybrid exchange entities.

candidates.headline: Q1 2026 shutdown roundups list Magic Eden wallet shutdown and multi-chain scale-back as part of a broader refocus.
candidates.hei_relevance: Likely outside HEI because this is wallet/NFT-marketplace infrastructure rather than CEX/DEX exchange entity. Mentioned only to avoid rediscovering it repeatedly.

duplicate_check.result: not checked in detail; likely out of scope

candidates.next_action: Hold as out-of-scope unless HEI expands to wallets/NFT tools.

candidates.headline: Q1 2026 shutdown roundups report Leap Wallet's full shutdown by late May 2026.
candidates.hei_relevance: Likely outside HEI because it is a wallet rather than an exchange, even if the shutdown is clear.

duplicate_check.result: not checked in detail; likely out of scope

candidates.next_action: Hold as out-of-scope unless HEI expands to wallets.

next_batch_recommendation.reason: Bithumb is a clear CEX operational incident with Reuters and Korean mainstream sources and is HEI-relevant as active-side CEX history. BlockFills is high-quality but outside CEX/DEX scope, while Magic Eden Wallet and Leap Wallet are wallet/product shutdowns rather than exchange records.
